triage.title: What's happening?
triage.subtitle: Tap what you see. We'll talk you through it, out loud, one step at a time.
triage.pinned: {{name}} has an allergy on file — tap here first
triage.disclaimer: For first-aid reference only. If your child is in immediate danger, contact your local emergency number.

guided.completeBody: Keep going until help arrives or your child recovers and is breathing normally.

scenes.checkInfant: Tap the foot · Watch the chest · 10 seconds
scenes.checkChild: Tap the shoulder · Call their name · Watch the chest
scenes.layFlat: Firm flat surface · Floor is best
scenes.placeTwoFinger: 2 fingers · Centre of chest · Below nipple line
scenes.placeHeel: Heel of one hand · Centre of chest · Arms straight
scenes.pushInfant: Push 4 cm deep · Let the chest rise back
scenes.pushChild: Push 5 cm deep · Shoulders above your hands
scenes.beat30: 30 pushes · Follow the beat · Count out loud
scenes.airway: Tilt the head back gently · Lift the chin
scenes.breathsInfant: Cover mouth AND nose · 2 small puffs · Watch the chest rise
scenes.breathsChild: Pinch the nose · Seal the mouth · 1 second per breath
scenes.recovery: Roll onto the side · Head tilted back · Stay with them
scenes.forearmHold: Face-down on your forearm · Head lower than chest · Brace on your thigh
scenes.backBlows: Heel of hand · Between the shoulder blades · 5 firm blows
scenes.flipFaceUp: Turn together as one · Keep the head low
scenes.chestThrusts: 2 fingers · Centre of chest · Slower and deeper than CPR
scenes.mouthCheck: Look first · Remove only what you can see · Never sweep blindly
scenes.cycle55: 5 back blows · 5 chest thrusts · Repeat until it comes out
scenes.goCprInfant: Gone limp? · Start Infant CPR now
scenes.childLean: Stand behind · Support the chest · Lean them well forward
scenes.fistNavel: Fist, thumb side in · Just above the navel · Other hand wraps over
scenes.heimlich: Pull sharply in and up · Each thrust a separate attempt
scenes.goCprChild: Gone limp? · Lower them gently · Start Child CPR
scenes.allergySigns: Swelling · Hives · Hard breathing — act now
scenes.epiGrip: Hold like a fist · Orange tip down · Never touch the tip
scenes.epiCap: Pull the blue cap straight off · Blue to the sky
scenes.epiInject: Orange to the thigh · Press until it clicks · Through clothes is OK
scenes.epiRub: Pull straight out · Rub for 10 seconds
scenes.callHelp: Call emergency services · Say what you gave and when
scenes.legsRaised: Lay them flat · Raise the legs · Never stand them up
scenes.epiSecond: No better in 5 minutes? · Second dose, other thigh
scenes.goHospital: Go to hospital · Even if they seem fine
scenes.waterRescue: Reach with a pole or rope · Never swim out yourself
scenes.breaths5: Open the airway · 5 rescue breaths · Then start CPR
scenes.keepWarm: Remove wet clothes · Cover with a blanket
scenes.handBarrier: Gloves or a plastic bag · Between hand and wound
scenes.pressWound: Press hard on the wound · Do not lift to peek
scenes.layerCloth: Soaked through? · New cloth ON TOP · Never remove the first
scenes.raiseLimb: Lift the limb above the heart · Keep pressing
scenes.noTourniquet: No tourniquet · Firm pressure does the job
scenes.cutPower: Switch off the mains · Or unplug it
scenes.noTouchLive: Do NOT touch them · Push the source away with dry wood
scenes.burnMarks: Look for entry and exit marks · Cover loosely
scenes.seizureCalm: Stay calm · Note the start time · Most stop within 2 minutes
scenes.clearArea: Move hard objects away · Cushion under the head
scenes.noRestrain: Do NOT hold them down · Let it pass
scenes.noMouth: NOTHING in the mouth · They cannot swallow their tongue
scenes.restAfter: Sleepiness is normal · Let them rest · Stay close
scenes.callDoctor: Call your doctor · Every first seizure needs review
scenes.stopBurning: Away from the source · Smother flames with a blanket
scenes.coolWater: Cool running water · The full 20 minutes
scenes.removeTight: Slide off jewellery and tight clothes · Before swelling starts
scenes.noRemedies: No ice · No butter · No toothpaste — only water
scenes.clingFilm: Lay cling film loosely over · Never wrap tight
scenes.keepWarmSips: Cover unburned areas · Offer small sips of water
scenes.headStill: Keep the head still · Wait for help if a bad fall
scenes.warningSigns: Vomiting · Confusion · Unequal pupils — get help
scenes.coldCompress: Wrapped cold pack · 10 minutes · Don't press hard
scenes.watchOver: Watch for 24–48 hours · Wake gently to check at night
scenes.noMeds: No sleep medicines · No strong painkillers
scenes.noVomit: Do NOT make them vomit · It causes more damage
scenes.wipeMouth: Wipe out the mouth · With a wet cloth
scenes.keepContainer: Keep the bottle or plant · Bring it to hospital
scenes.noteTime: Note the time and amount · Watch the symptoms
scenes.rinseSkin: Rinse skin 20 minutes · Remove soaked clothing
scenes.rinseEye: Hold the eye open · Rinse 20 minutes · Then hospital
scenes.getShade: Out of the heat · Into shade or cooling
scenes.fanCool: Wet the skin · Fan it · Cool packs at neck and groin
scenes.coolSips: Awake? · Small frequent sips · No fizzy drinks
scenes.warmSlowly: Too cold? · Dry blankets · Warm slowly
scenes.noDirectHeat: No hot pads or fires · Rapid rewarming is dangerous
scenes.warmSips: Alert? · Sips of a warm sweet drink
scenes.takeTemp: Digital thermometer · Under the arm for infants
scenes.dressLight: One light layer · Never bundle up a fever
scenes.offerFluids: Small sips, often · Breast milk for infants
scenes.treatChild: Happy and playing? · Maybe no medicine needed
scenes.noAspirin: NEVER aspirin for children · Paracetamol instead
scenes.spongeBath: Lukewarm water only · Stop if they shiver
scenes.washWound: Soap and running water · Full 5 minutes
scenes.coverDressing: Clean dry dressing · Not sealed tight
scenes.scrapeSting: Scrape sideways with a card · Never squeeze with tweezers
scenes.tickPull: Fine tweezers at the skin · Pull straight up · Don't twist
scenes.watchAllergy: Spreading swelling or hives? · Treat as anaphylaxis
